questions.question:
A PVC access-mode diagnostics brief is incomplete and contains stale or unsafe remediation guidance.

A ConfigMap named `rwop-diagnostics-brief` already exists in namespace `rwop-lab`. Repair it so that:
- `targetClaim` is `data-claim`
- `claimInventory` is `kubectl get pvc data-claim -n rwop-lab -o wide`
- `accessModeCheck` is `kubectl get pvc data-claim -n rwop-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.accessModes[0]}'`
- `storageClassCheck` is `kubectl get pvc data-claim -n rwop-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.storageClassName}'`
- `volumeNameCheck` is `kubectl get pvc data-claim -n rwop-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.volumeName}'`
- `readerPodCheck` is `kubectl get pod rwop-reader -n rwop-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.volumes[0].persistentVolumeClaim.claimName}'`
- `mountPathCheck` is `kubectl get pod rwop-reader -n rwop-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].volumeMounts[0].mountPath}'`
- `storageClassExpansionCheck` is `kubectl get storageclass rwop-hostpath -o jsonpath='{.allowVolumeExpansion}'`
- `eventCheck` is `kubectl get events -n rwop-lab --sort-by=.lastTimestamp`
- `safeManifestNote` is `confirm PVC access mode, claim consumer, and mount path before changing workload or storage manifests`
- save the repaired ConfigMap manifest to `/tmp/exam/q2801/rwop-diagnostics-brief.yaml`
- save a plain-text checklist to `/tmp/exam/q2801/rwop-diagnostics-checklist.txt` with the sections `Claim Inventory`, `Access Mode Checks`, and `Safe Manifest Review`

Do not delete the PVC, do not delete pods, and do not patch the live PVC or Pod during this drill.

verification.description: The RWOP diagnostics brief ConfigMap uses the intended claim target, exact PVC and consumer inspection commands, event evidence, and safe manifest guidance
The exported checklist contains the required sections and exact PVC access mode, consumer, and mount inspection commands
The repaired ConfigMap manifest is exported and stale unsafe PVC remediation guidance is removed
